title: Canopy Growth: BioSteel Funding Ceased To Stem Cash Burn
text: BioSteel's operations were a significant drag on Canopy Growth's bottom line and cash flow, with 60% of the adjusted EBITDA loss in the first quarter being due to BioSteel. In September 2023, Canopy Growth ceased funding BioSteel Sports Nutrition Inc. This filing immediately eliminates significant cash burn for Canopy and provides for an orderly realization of value of BioSteel's assets through a sale process. Despite initial growth, the segment was unprofitable. In the first quarter, BioSteel had a gross margin of negative 24%, nearly twice as bad as that of a year ago. Canopy Growth found material misstatements in BioSteel's previous financials, with overpaying for bonuses to minority shareholders as BioSteel's revenue was overstated. As a result, Canopy had to shave off CA$10 million of its net revenue for fiscal 2022, representing 2% of its top line. Two sale transactions transferring the assets of BioSteel Canada and BioSteel Manufacturing were completed for aggregate gross proceeds of $30.4 million.
